plain_explanation: NO-GO — LP-1 SENTINEL at SEVERE (score 70/100, GPS error 2.1 m). Postpone or re-route.
recommended_actions: Southward Bz (-50.0 nT) — conditions may intensify.
Activate backup navigation. Verify SATCOM link before departure.

data_quality.label: HIGH
notes: REPLAY — historical storm conditions (May 2024 Gannon storm (G5) — peak measured conditions), measured values, not live data.
NOAA SWPC G5 event reports, May 2024 (Gannon storm); GOES-16 XRS + DSCOVR measurements

equipment.likelihood: SEVERE — basis: observed Kp 9.0 (G5), GOES X-ray flux 5.8e-04 W/m² (X-class), proton flux 208 pfu (S2+).

findings.nomenclature: DAGR, Group 1 UAS nav, commercial L1 receivers
findings.effect: Position errors up to 100 m horizontal / 200 m vertical documented under severe geomagnetic storming.
findings.action: Do not use single-frequency GPS coordinates for precision fires or landing zones; verify by map/terrain or survey-grade receiver.
findings.citation: ALSSA, "True Impacts of Space Weather on a Ground Force"

findings.nomenclature: RQ-11 Raven, Skydio X2D, sUAS quadcopters
findings.effect: Hover and loiter operations high risk — potential loss of position hold and flyaway under GPS errors of 50–100 m.
findings.action: Advance or delay the flight window to quieter conditions; if the window cannot move, fly manual control throughout and disable autonomous RTH.
findings.citation: ALSSA, "True Impacts of Space Weather on a Ground Force"

unaffected.why: 30–88 MHz line-of-sight propagation never transits the ionosphere (60–1000 km altitude) — geomagnetic and ionospheric disturbances cannot reach it. Range-limited, but reliable.

inputs_echo.scenario_title: May 2024 Gannon storm (G5) — peak measured conditions
